prompt:
Ticket CHK-482, checkout totals. totals.ts currently does flat-rate shipping and taxes the whole cart — both are now wrong for us.

1) Tiered shipping. PricingConfig grows an optional shippingTiers: Array<{ minSubtotalCents, costCents }>. Pick the tier with the LARGEST minimum the order reaches, judged on the discounted subtotal (post-discount, pre-tax); reaching a minimum exactly counts. Config authors are merchants, so don't trust the array: it can arrive in any order (sort it yourself), but a tier set with no zero-minimum tier or with two tiers sharing a minimum is a config bug — throw. Empty order never pays shipping. No shippingTiers key at all -> flat rate exactly as today.

2) Tax-exempt categories. Optional taxExemptCategories: string[] on the config; line items whose category is listed stay out of the tax base. The subtle part finance signed off on: when there's an order-level discount, prorate it — the tax base is taxableSubtotal - round(discountCents * taxableSubtotal / subtotalCents), then apply taxRate and round. Fully exempt cart -> zero tax regardless of rate.

test_shipping_tax.ts encodes all of the above including the exact rounding (run node --test test_totals.ts test_shipping_tax.ts). test_totals.ts is the regression suite for current behavior; both files are off-limits to edits. Keep everything integer cents.
